The reviewed helper deliberately permits either or both optional filters and passes an empty request to query the caller's authorized roster scope.

Search text may be supplied as positional keyword or public --query; hidden --keyword remains a runtime compatibility alias and is intentionally not published as a parameter. The versioned binding owns query -> keyword.
The reviewed CLI accepts either a fuzzy keyword or an exact employee number, represented by a typed require-one-of constraint rather than two unconditional requirements.
The reviewed patch command accepts any non-empty subset of mutable template fields; a typed require-one-of constraint owns that requirement.
The reviewed optional --to-chat switch defaults to false and only controls post-submit delivery; it is not required to create a report.

The reviewed Cobra contract makes --off an optional mode switch: omission adds members to the mute list, while --off removes them. The shared MCP schema incorrectly marks the boolean as unconditionally required.
